data.title: Is WeShop the next big meme stock?
data.description:
WeShop (WSHP) is creating some buzz after its direct listing on the Nasdaq earlier in the month.

Notably, the Virgin Islands-based company announced its entry into the U.S. market this week with the launch of a unique shopping app ahead of the 2025 holiday season. The company said the app enables users to earn equity in WeShop (WSHP) itself via its ShareBack program, where purchases and referrals accumulate WePoints that can be converted into company shares. 

WeShop’s retail partnerships span hundreds of major U.S. brands, including Bloomingdale's, Walmart (WMT), and Nike (NKE), with over 1 billion products accessible through the platform. 

After a successful UK pilot generating over $140 million in sales, WeShop (WSHP) said it aims to transform shopping into a community-driven experience that rewards user participation and peer-to-peer recommendations. 

The social investing aspect of WeShop (WSHP) and share price surge has it looking to be a meme-like stock fueled by retail investor excitement. The name is not yet covered by the sell-side analyst community, but Seeking Alpha analyst Seeking Profits has a Strong Sell rating on WSHP.

WeShop (WSHP) was up 7.3% in premarket trading on Friday to $166.00 after a 22.6% bruising on Thursday. Trading on WeShop (WSHP) opened at $20.02 on November 14 after the company's direct listing on the Nasdaq and peaked at $250.00 on November 19.

data.title: OKYO Pharm Chairman and Founder acquires shares
data.description:
• OKYO Pharma (NASDAQ: OKYO) announced  on Friday that Gabriele Cerrone, Executive Chairman, has acquired 0.82M of the company’s ordinary shares on NASDAQ, bringing his total holding to 10.46M shares.
  • Cerrone has a beneficial interest in the Panetta Partners.
  • Shares -5.45%.

data.title: Tango Therapeutics launches $100M at-the-market stock offering
data.description:
• Tango Therapeutics (TNGX) entered a sales agreement with Leerink Partners to sell up to $100M of common stock through an at-the-market offering.
  • Leerink Partners will use commercially reasonable efforts to sell shares and will receive up to a 3% commission on gross proceeds.

data.title: UK unveils AI-related billions of pounds of investment to boost businesses and jobs
data.description:
The U.K. government said thousands of new jobs and billions of pounds of investment in AI have been announced across the U.K. as a driver of growth.

The government noted that international companies are also hosting new center in the U.K. and British companies are increasing investment – with £24.25B in private investment committed in this last month alone.

Companies like Vantage Data Centers and Microsoft (MSFT) are also collaborating with the government to deliver another AI Growth Zone. Set in South Wales, £10B of investment will unlock at least 5,000 jobs, including in industrial heartlands like the former Ford Bridgend Engine Plant.

"We’re proud to be investing in South Wales and playing our part to deliver this new AI Growth Zone," said Hugh Milward, vice president, External Affairs, Microsoft U.K.

The government added that AI Growth Zones will help transform local economies, with £5M of government funding for each Growth Zone to boost business adoption and invest in the skills of local workers.

The government said it will act as a “first customer” for promising U.K. start-ups which are building high-quality AI hardware products but currently struggle to get off the ground without investment. The new “advance market commitment” is backed up by up to £100M of government support to help British startups win customers in a global market.

AI Growth Zone data centers will house billions of pounds of hardware, according to the government.

Venture capitalist James Wise will chair the Sovereign AI Unit, backed by almost £500M in investment to help build and scale AI capabilities in Britain.

The unit will bring together government, industry and investors to provide funding for high potential start-ups and scale-ups in the U.K.

In addition, the government said that the process to spend up to £250M on compute has been launched.

A new strategy is also being unveiled to make sure AI boosts scientific discovery backed by up to £137M in government funding. Its first mission will be focused on using AI to speed up the research of new drugs and treatments.

The government said that several sites will make up a new AI Growth Zone in South Wales, dedicated areas of AI development, stretching along the M4 corridor from Newport to Bridgend and has the potential to harness over 1 GW by early 2030s.

The government added that building on these partnerships, Nvidia"s (NVDA) continued investments and collaborations are helping strengthen the U.K’s role as a global hub for AI development.

Four AI Growth Zone sites have now been announced in the 11 months since the launch of the country's AI Opportunities Action Plan.

American AI inference platform Groq will be opening its first U.K. data center in London this year, backed by around £100M.

Graphcore and SoftBank have renewed their backing for the U.K. with a new AI development lab in Bristol, doubling their headcount to 750 jobs.

AI Pathfinder is fast-tracking U.K. sovereign AI with a £150M GPU deployment in Northamptonshire. This is the first step in an £18B program into U.K. sovereign AI infrastructure over the next five years.

Perplexity AI is investing £80M to expand its offices in London, creating 100 jobs, according to the government.

The British government noted that these fresh investments build on Equinix’s recent £4B commitment to constructing and operating a high-performance data center campus in Hertfordshire, supporting thousands of jobs. Zoom had also confirmed plans to open a new U.K. data center in 2026 backed by £24M investment over the next three years.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Meta said to get into power trading to fuel its AI energy needs
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is entering the electricity trading market to accelerate the construction of new U.S. power plants vital to its artificial intelligence ambitions, according to a report by Bloomberg on Friday.

The move into power trading comes after Meta heard from investors and plant developers that too few power buyers were willing to make the early, long-term commitments required to spur investment.

According to the company's head of global energy, Urvi Parekh, trading electricity will give the company flexibility to enter longer contracts, which plant developers need to secure investment.

Parekh expects Meta to ramp up by leveraging external partners to help the company learn the business before it tries to enter electricity markets as a standalone entity, the report said.

Meta's move into trading underscores the huge increase in power demand transforming the U.S. energy and electricity market, with tech companies racing to develop advanced AI systems that use massive amounts of electricity, the report said.

Meta declined to provide a timeline for the trading operations or further details on the expected structure for its trading.

Based on projections from BloombergNEF, power demand from data centers used to build and run AI models is set to quadruple in 10 years.

data.title: Dallas Fed's Logan wants to pause rate cuts until more progress made on inflation
data.description:
Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan called for the Federal Reserve to pause further rate cuts until the central bank has more evidence to justify such a move, she said on Friday. 

Even in September, she wasn't sure there was room to cut rates more than once or twice and still keep monetary policy at a restrictive stance. Logan is not a voting member of the Federal Open Market Committee this year, but she will be in 2026.

The FOMC proceeded with 25-basis-point rate cuts at both its September and October meetings, bringing the federal funds rate target range to 3.75%-4.00%.

"And having made two cuts, I’m not certain we have room for more. Monetary policy works with a lag," Logan said. "It’s too soon to directly assess the degree of restriction from the current stance of policy, with two rate cuts already on board."

She said being patient can help the Fed avoid missteps in setting rates.

"Taking the time to learn more can help us avoid unnecessary reversals that might generate unwanted financial and economic volatility," Logan said.

Her comments contrast with those of New York Fed President John Williams, who said he sees room to make further rate adjustments in the near term, hinting that he'd be open to a rate cut in December. The minutes from the FOMC's Oct. 28-29 meeting showed the widening spectrum of views held by the policymakers, with a divide between those who wanted to hold, those who wanted to cut by 25 bps, and one who wanted by cut by 50 bps.

Logan also spoke about the Fed's decision to end shrinking its balance sheet. Ending its asset runoff slows, but doesn't stop, the decline in reserves, she explained.

"If the Fed’s assets are held fixed, trend growth in non-reserve liabilities such as currency will absorb more of the balance sheet over time," she said. "The demand for reserves will likely also change over time in response to economic growth, changes in the banking and payments businesses, and adjustments in regulation."

To keep reserves at ample levels, the FOMC will need to decide when to start adding to assets, Logan noted. Looking at where market rates are settling, she expects it "will not be long" before it's appropriate to "resume balance sheet growth so that money market rates can average close to, but perhaps slightly below, IORB (interest rate on reserve balances)."

data.title: Bulls retreat from Bath & Body Works after a rough quarter
data.description:
Bath & Body Works (BBWI) saw a wave of downgrades on Friday after the retailer lowered its full-year profit guidance amid what it said has been a continuation of macro consumer pressures. During the earnings call, BBWI management noted that merchandise margin was negatively impacted by approximately $35 million, or roughly 200 basis points, from tariffs. The company also increased its promotional activity to clear seasonal product.

Firms lowering the ratings boom included Morgan Stanley (Equal Weight from Overweight), Telsey Advisory (Market Perform from Outperform), Goldman Sachs (Neutral from Buy), and Robert Baird (Neutral from Outperform).

Morgan Stanley analyst Alex Straton warned that the path for Bath & Body Works (BBWI) to consistently see solid sales growth and margin expansion appears to be increasingly unreachable. "Potentially structural topline headwinds & elevated investment mean BBWI could face ongoing topline declines & margin erosion from here," warned Straton.

Shares of Bath & Body Works (BBWI) trickled 0.8% lower after a 24.8% slide on Thursday that sent the stock to a new 52-week low of $15.23.

data.title: Heliostar Metals GAAP EPS of $0.01, revenue of $26.76M
data.description:
• Heliostar Metals press release (HSTXF): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.01.
  • Revenue of $26.76M.
  • The company said it is on track to achieve its annual production guidance of 31,000 to 41,000 GEOs, annual cash cost of $1,800-1,900 per GEO sold and AISC of $1,950-2,100 per GEO sold for 2025.

data.title: Japan approves restart of world's biggest nuclear power plant
data.description:
The world's biggest nuclear power plant is poised to restart after a Japanese regional governor cleared plans Friday to proceed with two reactors at Tokyo Electric's (OTCPK:TKECF) (OTCPK:TKECY) Kashiwazaki Kariwa plant.

The decision is the strongest signal yet that Japan is re-embracing nuclear power after the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi power plant meltdown, the world’s worst nuclear disaster since Chernobyl; after the disaster, Japan shut all 54 of its nuclear reactors in operation, leaving it heavily reliant on fossil fuel imports.

Japan spent ¥10.7 trillion ($68 billion) last year on imported liquefied natural gas and coal, 10% of its total import costs.

Once approved by the Niigata prefectural assembly, the decision would cover reactors No.6 and No.7, the two biggest at the Kashiwazaki Kariwa plant. 

As electricity demand is set to grow due to data center and semiconductor business expansion, "it would be difficult to stop something that passed the country's regulatory standards without any rational reason," Prefecture Governor Hideyo Hanazumi said.

ETFs: (NLR), (URA), (URNM)

data.title: Magna International and GAC to assemble AION V in Europe
data.description:
• Canada’s Magna International (MGA) and China’s GAC will together assemble GAC’s electric SUV, AION V, at Magna’s (MGA) assembly plant in Graz, Austria, accelerating GAC’s expansion in Europe and Magna’s (MGA) manufacturing capabilities.
  • Through partnerships such as its deal with Magna (MGA), Guangzhou-based GAC aims to broaden its European footprint beyond Finland, Portugal, and Poland — and sidestep EU tariffs on Chinese-built vehicles by assembling cars within Europe.
  • Magna (MGA) currently assembles the Xpeng (XPEV) G6 and G9 and Mercedes (OTCPK:MBGAF) (OTCPK:MBGYY) G-Class at its Graz facility, as well as ICE vehicles, the BMW (OTC:BMWKY) Z4 and Toyota (TM) Supra.  
  • Magna International (MGA) shares are catching a modest bid on the news and are set to snap a 6 day losing streak at Friday’s open.
  • Source: Press Release
